text.title: AR Design Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the AR Design Patterns methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: AR UI elements MUST anchor to a tracked surface or persistent feature; floating world-locked text without anchor stabilisation is forbidden.

rationale.source: Apple HIG: AR + Google AR Design Guidelines

rule.rationale: Drifting AR text is unreadable for users with motor or visual impairments.

rule.statement: Contrast tokens MUST adapt to detected environment luminance: outdoor / sun → ≥7:1; indoor / standard → ≥4.5:1; dark → ≥3:1 with halo border.
rule.rationale: Static contrast tokens fail in bright outdoor scenes.

rule.statement: If interaction uses dwell or gaze input, a discrete trigger (button / pinch / voice) alternate MUST also be available.
rule.rationale: Dwell exhausts users with eye tracking conditions; alternative input is required.

rule.statement: Critical AR notifications MUST emit a spatial-audio cue with direction matching the visual element.
rule.rationale: Blind / low-vision users navigate AR via audio; non-spatial beep is insufficient.

rule.statement: Cumulative animation > 30s/min OR rapid scene transitions > 1 per 5s require a `reduced-motion` alternate showing static placeholders.
rule.rationale: Vestibular sensitivity is the leading AR drop-off cause.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent's context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-ar-design-patterns.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
